own_review_points: Caching.setDefaultClassLoader is JVM-global static; the get-old/set-new save in prepareForUse and restore in releaseFromUse is non-atomic and corrupts state under concurrent multi-SessionFactory init/close
preInitializedCacheManager is never nulled in releaseFromUse (cacheManager and oldJsr107CacheClassLoader are reset, it is not)
getServiceClassLoader uses getService (may return null) rather than requireService (human suggestion); workWithClassLoader(cl->cl) identity lambda is an odd getter use

Design (human): most JCacheRegionFactory changes exist only to keep tests passing; prefer changing tests to inject CACHE_MANAGER and deprecate/move JCacheHelper to test sources rather than using it in main
notes: Strong lead point: v8 correctly identifies the non-atomic save/restore of JVM-global Caching.setDefaultClassLoader as a concurrency hazard (critical) and aligns with the human's 'is it necessary to keep the old classloader' concern, going deeper. getCachingProvider(null) spec concern and the workWithClassLoader identity-lambda oddity are grounded good points; preInitializedCacheManager-not-cleared is verified. EhCache-comment rewording and the test-cleanup praise are trivial. Missed: the getService->requireService null suggestion and the overarching design critique (revert most main-code changes, deprecate JCacheHelper). Solid review.
